1	# node-selection-enforcement Delta
2	
3	## ADDED Requirements
4	
5	### Requirement: Boundary-crossing selections escalate to whole sibling subtrees
6	In outline mode, when a `selection-only` transaction contains a non-empty range whose
7	anchor and head resolve to different nodes of the parsed tree, the filter SHALL replace
8	that range with the minimal contiguous cover of whole sibling subtrees: the run of
9	children of the ends' deepest common ancestor scope that spans both ends, from the first
10	subtree's first character to the last subtree's last character. A node is never
11	partially selected together with content outside it.
12	
13	#### Scenario: Drag from mid-paragraph into the next paragraph
14	- **WHEN** the user drag-selects from the middle of one paragraph node into the middle
15	  of the next sibling paragraph
16	- **THEN** the selection becomes both paragraphs in full
17	
18	#### Scenario: Selection leaving a parent covers its subtree
19	- **WHEN** a selection starts inside a heading's text and ends inside a paragraph
20	  within that heading's section
21	- **THEN** the selection covers the heading's entire subtree (the heading line and all
22	  nodes in its section)
23	
24	#### Scenario: Keyboard selection crossing a boundary
25	- **WHEN** the user extends a selection with Shift+ArrowDown from inside one node into
26	  the next node
27	- **THEN** the resulting selection covers both nodes' subtrees in full
28	
29	### Requirement: Within-node selections and cursors are untouched
30	Selection ranges whose two ends resolve to the same node SHALL pass through unmodified,
31	and empty ranges (cursors) SHALL never be moved by this layer, so all within-node text
32	selection (words, phrases, partial lines) and cursor placement remain byte-for-byte
33	native.
34	
35	#### Scenario: Double-click word selection
36	- **WHEN** the user double-clicks a word inside a node
37	- **THEN** the native word selection is applied unmodified
38	
39	#### Scenario: Cursor placement is never escalated
40	- **WHEN** the user clicks to place the cursor anywhere in an outline-mode note
41	- **THEN** the cursor lands exactly where stock Obsidian would place it
42	
43	### Requirement: Escalation preserves orientation and transaction integrity
44	An escalated range SHALL preserve the original anchor/head orientation (backward
45	selections stay backward), and the replacement SHALL occur within the same transaction
46	via the filter's return value — no additional dispatch, no history entry, and no
47	observable intermediate selection state.
48	
49	#### Scenario: Backward drag stays backward
50	- **WHEN** the user drags a selection upward from a lower node into an upper node
51	- **THEN** the escalated selection covers both subtrees with the head at the start side
52	
53	#### Scenario: Escalation during live drag is stable
54	- **WHEN** the user drags across a node boundary and continues dragging
55	- **THEN** each pointer update yields the escalated selection without flicker between
56	  native and escalated states
57	
58	### Requirement: Multi-range selections escalate per range
59	For multi-cursor/multi-range selections, each non-empty range SHALL be escalated
60	independently under the same rules, with standard `EditorSelection` normalization
61	merging any overlapping results.
62	
63	#### Scenario: Two ranges, one crossing a boundary
64	- **WHEN** a multi-range selection has one within-node range and one boundary-crossing
65	  range
66	- **THEN** the within-node range is untouched and the crossing range escalates
67	
68	### Requirement: Preamble and out-of-jurisdiction selections pass through
69	Selection ranges with either end in the document preamble (frontmatter or other content
70	before the first node) SHALL pass through unmodified. Select All SHALL behave exactly as
71	stock Obsidian.
72	
73	#### Scenario: Select All is native
74	- **WHEN** the user presses the Select All shortcut in an outline-mode note with
75	  frontmatter
76	- **THEN** the entire document is selected exactly as in stock Obsidian
77	
78	### Requirement: Enforcement is scoped to outline mode and enforced classes only
79	Selection escalation SHALL apply only in outline-mode editors and only to transactions
80	classified `selection-only`. Transactions of every other class — including
81	`programmatic`, `composition`, and `plugin-own` — SHALL keep their selections untouched,
82	and off-mode notes SHALL show byte-for-byte stock selection behavior.
83	
84	#### Scenario: Off-mode drag selection is native
85	- **WHEN** the user drag-selects across paragraphs in a note without outline mode
86	- **THEN** the selection is exactly the native character-level selection
87	
88	#### Scenario: Programmatic selection restore is untouched
89	- **WHEN** a transaction without a user event restores a mid-node selection (e.g.
90	  workspace restore)
91	- **THEN** the selection is applied exactly as dispatched, even if it crosses node
92	  boundaries
93

file.content:
# node-selection-enforcement Delta

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Boundary-crossing selections escalate to whole sibling subtrees
In outline mode, when a `selection-only` transaction contains a non-empty range whose
anchor and head resolve to different nodes of the parsed tree, the filter SHALL replace
that range with the minimal contiguous cover of whole sibling subtrees: the run of
children of the ends' deepest common ancestor scope that spans both ends, from the first
subtree's first character to the last subtree's last character. A node is never
partially selected together with content outside it.

#### Scenario: Drag from mid-paragraph into the next paragraph
- **WHEN** the user drag-selects from the middle of one paragraph node into the middle
  of the next sibling paragraph
- **THEN** the selection becomes both paragraphs in full

#### Scenario: Selection leaving a parent covers its subtree
- **WHEN** a selection starts inside a heading's text and ends inside a paragraph
  within that heading's section
- **THEN** the selection covers the heading's entire subtree (the heading line and all
  nodes in its section)

#### Scenario: Keyboard selection crossing a boundary
- **WHEN** the user extends a selection with Shift+ArrowDown from inside one node into
  the next node
- **THEN** the resulting selection covers both nodes' subtrees in full

### Requirement: Within-node selections and cursors are untouched
Selection ranges whose two ends resolve to the same node SHALL pass through unmodified,
and empty ranges (cursors) SHALL never be moved by this layer, so all within-node text
selection (words, phrases, partial lines) and cursor placement remain byte-for-byte
native.

#### Scenario: Double-click word selection
- **WHEN** the user double-clicks a word inside a node
- **THEN** the native word selection is applied unmodified

#### Scenario: Cursor placement is never escalated
- **WHEN** the user clicks to place the cursor anywhere in an outline-mode note
- **THEN** the cursor lands exactly where stock Obsidian would place it

### Requirement: Escalation preserves orientation and transaction integrity
An escalated range SHALL preserve the original anchor/head orientation (backward
selections stay backward), and the replacement SHALL occur within the same transaction
via the filter's return value — no additional dispatch, no history entry, and no
observable intermediate selection state.

#### Scenario: Backward drag stays backward
- **WHEN** the user drags a selection upward from a lower node into an upper node
- **THEN** the escalated selection covers both subtrees with the head at the start side

#### Scenario: Escalation during live drag is stable
- **WHEN** the user drags across a node boundary and continues dragging
- **THEN** each pointer update yields the escalated selection without flicker between
  native and escalated states

### Requirement: Multi-range selections escalate per range
For multi-cursor/multi-range selections, each non-empty range SHALL be escalated
independently under the same rules, with standard `EditorSelection` normalization
merging any overlapping results.

#### Scenario: Two ranges, one crossing a boundary
- **WHEN** a multi-range selection has one within-node range and one boundary-crossing
  range
- **THEN** the within-node range is untouched and the crossing range escalates

### Requirement: Preamble and out-of-jurisdiction selections pass through
Selection ranges with either end in the document preamble (frontmatter or other content
before the first node) SHALL pass through unmodified. Select All SHALL behave exactly as
stock Obsidian.

#### Scenario: Select All is native
- **WHEN** the user presses the Select All shortcut in an outline-mode note with
  frontmatter
- **THEN** the entire document is selected exactly as in stock Obsidian

### Requirement: Enforcement is scoped to outline mode and enforced classes only
Selection escalation SHALL apply only in outline-mode editors and only to transactions
classified `selection-only`. Transactions of every other class — including
`programmatic`, `composition`, and `plugin-own` — SHALL keep their selections untouched,
and off-mode notes SHALL show byte-for-byte stock selection behavior.

#### Scenario: Off-mode drag selection is native
- **WHEN** the user drag-selects across paragraphs in a note without outline mode
- **THEN** the selection is exactly the native character-level selection

#### Scenario: Programmatic selection restore is untouched
- **WHEN** a transaction without a user event restores a mid-node selection (e.g.
  workspace restore)
- **THEN** the selection is applied exactly as dispatched, even if it crosses node
  boundaries

content.content:
1	# transaction-classification Delta
2	
3	## ADDED Requirements
4	
5	### Requirement: Enforcement funnel is registered and scoped to outline mode
6	A CM6 `transactionFilter` SHALL be registered via `registerEditorExtension` and SHALL
7	inspect every transaction dispatched in any editor. For editors whose file does not have
8	outline mode enabled (resolved through the public `editorInfoField`), the filter SHALL
9	return the transaction unchanged with no other observable effect, so off-mode editor
10	behavior is byte-for-byte and timing-indistinguishable from stock Obsidian.
11	
12	#### Scenario: Off-mode transaction untouched
13	- **WHEN** any edit or selection change is made in a note without outline mode
14	- **THEN** the dispatched transaction is applied exactly as produced, and no
15	  classification side effects (stats, logs) are recorded for it
16	
17	#### Scenario: Mode toggle takes effect immediately
18	- **WHEN** outline mode is toggled on for the active note
19	- **THEN** the very next transaction in that editor is classified, with no editor reload
20	
21	### Requirement: Every transaction receives exactly one classification
22	In outline-mode editors the filter SHALL assign each transaction exactly one class from:
23	`programmatic`, `composition`, `plugin-own`, `selection-only`, `within-node-edit`,
24	`boundary-crossing-edit` — evaluated in that order, first match wins. Classification
25	SHALL be computed by a pure function over transaction facts and the parsed tree, unit-
26	and property-tested independently of Obsidian. Any transaction not confidently matching
27	an enforced class SHALL pass through unmodified (default-permit).
28	
29	#### Scenario: Typing inside a node
30	- **WHEN** the user types a character in the middle of a paragraph node's text
31	- **THEN** the transaction is classified `within-node-edit` and applied unmodified
32	
33	#### Scenario: Edit spanning two nodes counted but not altered
34	- **WHEN** a deletion's change range starts inside one node and ends inside the next
35	- **THEN** the transaction is classified `boundary-crossing-edit`, counted in the stats
36	  surface, and applied unmodified (rewriting is out of scope for this change)
37	
38	### Requirement: Document text is never modified by this layer
39	The classification layer SHALL NOT add, remove, or alter any change spec of any
40	transaction, in any class, under any condition. Its only permitted transaction
41	modification is the selection replacement defined by the node-selection-enforcement
42	capability.
43	
44	#### Scenario: Byte-identical buffer across classified edits
45	- **WHEN** any sequence of edits is dispatched in an outline-mode note with the filter
46	  active
47	- **THEN** the resulting buffer is byte-identical to the same sequence applied with the
48	  filter absent
49	
50	### Requirement: Programmatic and remote transactions pass through untouched
51	Transactions carrying no `userEvent` annotation, or carrying undo/redo history
52	signatures, SHALL be classified `programmatic` and passed through with changes and
53	selection untouched. This SHALL hold for full-document loads and sync/external-reload
54	style replacements, preserving the interop guarantee that other tools' edits are never
55	fought or rewritten.
56	
57	#### Scenario: External-style full-document replacement
58	- **WHEN** the document is replaced programmatically (a `setValue`-style dispatch with
59	  no user event, as an external reload or sync would produce)
60	- **THEN** the transaction is classified `programmatic` and applied byte-identically,
61	  including its selection
62	
63	#### Scenario: Undo restores state without re-normalization
64	- **WHEN** the user invokes undo after any classified transaction
65	- **THEN** the history transaction passes through unmodified and restores the prior
66	  state exactly
67	
68	### Requirement: Grammar and command transactions are recognized as plugin-own
69	Transactions dispatched by this plugin's own keyboard grammar and structural commands
70	(identified by their `userEvent` annotations) SHALL be classified `plugin-own` and
71	passed through unmodified — they are valid by construction and MUST NOT be re-processed.
72	
73	#### Scenario: Tab indent is not reclassified
74	- **WHEN** Tab indents a node via the outline keyboard grammar
75	- **THEN** the resulting transaction is classified `plugin-own` and applied exactly as
76	  the grammar produced it, including its cursor placement
77	
78	### Requirement: IME composition is never interfered with
79	Transactions that are part of an active IME composition SHALL be classified
80	`composition` and passed through with changes and selection untouched, regardless of
81	what spans they touch.
82	
83	#### Scenario: Composition across a node boundary region
84	- **WHEN** an IME composition session is active in an outline-mode note
85	- **THEN** every composition transaction applies unmodified and composition completes
86	  exactly as in stock Obsidian
87	
88	### Requirement: Nested editors are safe from enforcement effects
89	Classification and any enforcement built on it SHALL produce no observable effect
90	inside Obsidian's nested per-cell editors (e.g. Live Preview table cells), whose
91	transactions also flow through globally registered extensions. Safety SHALL be
92	verified against the existing wide-table fixture, not assumed.
93	
94	#### Scenario: Editing a table cell in an outline-mode note
95	- **WHEN** the user types, selects, and drag-selects inside an actively edited table
96	  cell of an outline-mode note
97	- **THEN** cell content and cell selection behave byte-for-byte as stock Obsidian, with
98	  no escalation or other enforcement effect inside the cell
99	
100	### Requirement: Classification meets the keystroke-latency budget
101	Per-transaction classification cost (including tree access) SHALL stay within the
102	budget of ≤ 1 ms median and ≤ 8 ms p95 on a ~2000-line stress note, measured by the
103	instrumentation's own timing counters, with the measured evidence recorded in the
104	change's verification notes. Parsed-tree access SHALL be cached per document version so
105	selection-only transactions never re-parse.
106	
107	#### Scenario: Stress-note measurement recorded
108	- **WHEN** the evidence suite drives typing and selection across a ~2000-line note
109	- **THEN** recorded timings satisfy the budget, and the numbers (median/p95) are
110	  written into the change documentation
111	
112	### Requirement: Classification is observable for verification
113	The plugin SHALL maintain a classification stats surface (per-class counters, timing
114	aggregates, and a bounded ring buffer of recent classifications) readable by automated
115	tests through the plugin instance. Per-transaction console logging SHALL be gated
116	behind the existing parser-crosscheck debug setting, and a developer command SHALL
117	print a stats summary on demand. Every mutation path in the coverage matrix — typing,
118	paste, mouse drag selection, keyboard selection, find-and-replace, drag-drop text,
119	programmatic edit — SHALL have an automated scenario asserting its observed
120	classification, forming the permanent evidence that all mutation paths flow through
121	the funnel.
122	
123	#### Scenario: Paste is observed by the funnel
124	- **WHEN** multi-line text is pasted into an outline-mode note
125	- **THEN** the stats surface records a classification for the paste transaction
126	
127	#### Scenario: Debug logging stays opt-in
128	- **WHEN** the debug setting is off
129	- **THEN** no per-transaction console output is produced, while counters still update
130

# transaction-classification Delta

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Enforcement funnel is registered and scoped to outline mode
A CM6 `transactionFilter` SHALL be registered via `registerEditorExtension` and SHALL
inspect every transaction dispatched in any editor. For editors whose file does not have
outline mode enabled (resolved through the public `editorInfoField`), the filter SHALL
return the transaction unchanged with no other observable effect, so off-mode editor
behavior is byte-for-byte and timing-indistinguishable from stock Obsidian.

#### Scenario: Off-mode transaction untouched
- **WHEN** any edit or selection change is made in a note without outline mode
- **THEN** the dispatched transaction is applied exactly as produced, and no
  classification side effects (stats, logs) are recorded for it

#### Scenario: Mode toggle takes effect immediately
- **WHEN** outline mode is toggled on for the active note
- **THEN** the very next transaction in that editor is classified, with no editor reload

### Requirement: Every transaction receives exactly one classification
In outline-mode editors the filter SHALL assign each transaction exactly one class from:
`programmatic`, `composition`, `plugin-own`, `selection-only`, `within-node-edit`,
`boundary-crossing-edit` — evaluated in that order, first match wins. Classification
SHALL be computed by a pure function over transaction facts and the parsed tree, unit-
and property-tested independently of Obsidian. Any transaction not confidently matching
an enforced class SHALL pass through unmodified (default-permit).

#### Scenario: Typing inside a node
- **WHEN** the user types a character in the middle of a paragraph node's text
- **THEN** the transaction is classified `within-node-edit` and applied unmodified

#### Scenario: Edit spanning two nodes counted but not altered
- **WHEN** a deletion's change range starts inside one node and ends inside the next
- **THEN** the transaction is classified `boundary-crossing-edit`, counted in the stats
  surface, and applied unmodified (rewriting is out of scope for this change)

### Requirement: Document text is never modified by this layer
The classification layer SHALL NOT add, remove, or alter any change spec of any
transaction, in any class, under any condition. Its only permitted transaction
modification is the selection replacement defined by the node-selection-enforcement
capability.

#### Scenario: Byte-identical buffer across classified edits
- **WHEN** any sequence of edits is dispatched in an outline-mode note with the filter
  active
- **THEN** the resulting buffer is byte-identical to the same sequence applied with the
  filter absent

### Requirement: Programmatic and remote transactions pass through untouched
Transactions carrying no `userEvent` annotation, or carrying undo/redo history
signatures, SHALL be classified `programmatic` and passed through with changes and
selection untouched. This SHALL hold for full-document loads and sync/external-reload
style replacements, preserving the interop guarantee that other tools' edits are never
fought or rewritten.

#### Scenario: External-style full-document replacement
- **WHEN** the document is replaced programmatically (a `setValue`-style dispatch with
  no user event, as an external reload or sync would produce)
- **THEN** the transaction is classified `programmatic` and applied byte-identically,
  including its selection

#### Scenario: Undo restores state without re-normalization
- **WHEN** the user invokes undo after any classified transaction
- **THEN** the history transaction passes through unmodified and restores the prior
  state exactly

### Requirement: Grammar and command transactions are recognized as plugin-own
Transactions dispatched by this plugin's own keyboard grammar and structural commands
(identified by their `userEvent` annotations) SHALL be classified `plugin-own` and
passed through unmodified — they are valid by construction and MUST NOT be re-processed.

#### Scenario: Tab indent is not reclassified
- **WHEN** Tab indents a node via the outline keyboard grammar
- **THEN** the resulting transaction is classified `plugin-own` and applied exactly as
  the grammar produced it, including its cursor placement

### Requirement: IME composition is never interfered with
Transactions that are part of an active IME composition SHALL be classified
`composition` and passed through with changes and selection untouched, regardless of
what spans they touch.

#### Scenario: Composition across a node boundary region
- **WHEN** an IME composition session is active in an outline-mode note
- **THEN** every composition transaction applies unmodified and composition completes
  exactly as in stock Obsidian

### Requirement: Nested editors are safe from enforcement effects
Classification and any enforcement built on it SHALL produce no observable effect
inside Obsidian's nested per-cell editors (e.g. Live Preview table cells), whose
transactions also flow through globally registered extensions. Safety SHALL be
verified against the existing wide-table fixture, not assumed.

#### Scenario: Editing a table cell in an outline-mode note
- **WHEN** the user types, selects, and drag-selects inside an actively edited table
  cell of an outline-mode note
- **THEN** cell content and cell selection behave byte-for-byte as stock Obsidian, with
  no escalation or other enforcement effect inside the cell

### Requirement: Classification meets the keystroke-latency budget
Per-transaction classification cost (including tree access) SHALL stay within the
budget of ≤ 1 ms median and ≤ 8 ms p95 on a ~2000-line stress note, measured by the
instrumentation's own timing counters, with the measured evidence recorded in the
change's verification notes. Parsed-tree access SHALL be cached per document version so
selection-only transactions never re-parse.

#### Scenario: Stress-note measurement recorded
- **WHEN** the evidence suite drives typing and selection across a ~2000-line note
- **THEN** recorded timings satisfy the budget, and the numbers (median/p95) are
  written into the change documentation

### Requirement: Classification is observable for verification
The plugin SHALL maintain a classification stats surface (per-class counters, timing
aggregates, and a bounded ring buffer of recent classifications) readable by automated
tests through the plugin instance. Per-transaction console logging SHALL be gated
behind the existing parser-crosscheck debug setting, and a developer command SHALL
print a stats summary on demand. Every mutation path in the coverage matrix — typing,
paste, mouse drag selection, keyboard selection, find-and-replace, drag-drop text,
programmatic edit — SHALL have an automated scenario asserting its observed
classification, forming the permanent evidence that all mutation paths flow through
the funnel.

#### Scenario: Paste is observed by the funnel
- **WHEN** multi-line text is pasted into an outline-mode note
- **THEN** the stats surface records a classification for the paste transaction

#### Scenario: Debug logging stays opt-in
- **WHEN** the debug setting is off
- **THEN** no per-transaction console output is produced, while counters still update

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_automation_manage: Manage Devin automations — list, get, create, update, delete, or fetch the trigger event schemas (private mode only)
- devin_code_scan_manage: Manage Devin code scans — list scans, list findings, list profiles, get a profile (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)
- find_setting: Find Devin webapp settings pages and deep-link URLs for them (private mode only)

content.thinking:
I'm looking at a substantial refactoring effort spanning 26 tasks across six major areas—pure classifier, funnel adapter, Phase A evidence end-to-end suite, escalation core, escalation wiring, and verification/closure. Before diving into the implementation, I need to map out the existing codebase architecture to understand the patterns and dependencies across ops.ts, dispatch.ts, locate.ts, parse.ts, crosscheck.ts, main.ts, decorations, and the e2e test harness.

Given the scope and complexity—including real Obsidian e2e work, manual verification passes, and performance measurement—I can't complete this autonomously in one pass. I'll implement as much as possible programmatically but will pause at manual verification points like IME composition checks and manual test passes, then explore the repo structure to establish the conventions I need to follow.

content.thinking: I'm going to work through a comprehensive list of 26 tasks and dive deep into the codebase, starting with the core files like model.ts, ops.ts, parse.ts, and the others to understand how they all fit together.

I need to examine several key files to understand the architecture: decorations.ts for the CodeMirror 6 extension pattern, keymap.ts for grammar extensions, dispatch.ts for converting edits to changes, plus parse.ts, ops.ts, and mode-registry.ts to see how they fit together.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * CM6 adapter for outline mode's decorations. All the pure computation
3	 * (depth/supplementalDepth, per-line guide depths) lives in decorate.ts;
4	 * this module only turns those facts into CM6 decorations/DOM, gated
5	 * per-editor on outline mode via the public `editorInfoField` — same
6	 * gating pattern as keymap.ts's grammarExtension.
7	 *
8	 * Two mechanisms, because Obsidian renders atom kinds two different ways in
9	 * Live Preview:
10	 *
11	 * - Most lines (headings, paragraphs, list items, code fences, plain
12	 *   blockquotes) render as a real `.cm-line` that CM6 lets us decorate
13	 *   declaratively: one class plus one CSS custom property per fact (never
14	 *   an inline shorthand property), so styles.css owns the actual
15	 *   `padding-left`/`margin-left` rules and their units (additive-only
16	 *   indentation, Experiment 1) plus the guide-line gradient (Experiment 2b,
17	 *   see the "Guide lines" section below), PLUS a real CM6 `Decoration.widget`
18	 *   for the block marker icon (Experiment 5a, see below) — CM6's own,
19	 *   supported way to insert extra DOM into a line's content without
20	 *   fighting its own re-render/diffing.
21	 * - Tables, callouts, raw HTML blocks, and horizontal rules are rendered as
22	 *   opaque replacement widgets (`.cm-embed-block`, or `.hr` for the rule) —
23	 *   confirmed live: a `Decoration.line` targeting that line's position has
24	 *   no effect at all (not even a class-merge partial win), because the
25	 *   widget's own `toDOM()` produces the line's DOM wholesale and neither
26	 *   CM6 nor Obsidian threads our decoration's class/attributes through it.
27	 *   These need a direct, imperative DOM patch instead — a `ViewPlugin`
28	 *   that, after each render, sets `margin-left` inline (with `!important`,
29	 *   which always wins for an inline style regardless of what any
30	 *   stylesheet rule does), and appends a marker icon child, on whichever
31	 *   such widgets are currently mounted. Appending a child directly into one
32	 *   of these widgets is safe (proven across this experiment's e2e runs):
33	 *   they're opaque, Obsidian-owned subtrees CM6 never re-diffs internally.
34	 *   The equivalent is NOT safe for a plain `.cm-line`, which CM6 actively
35	 *   owns/re-renders — an earlier version of this code tried appending a
36	 *   marker child directly into `.cm-line`s from this same ViewPlugin (to
37	 *   get live-measured multi-line height for code fences) and it pegged
38	 *   Obsidian's renderer at 100%+ CPU indefinitely, almost certainly CM6's
39	 *   own DOM-mutation observer (used to detect external/IME edits) reacting
40	 *   to the unexpected child and re-triggering updates in a feedback loop.
41	 *   Reverted; plain-line markers stay on the CM6-native `Decoration.widget`
42	 *   path below, which has no such risk.
43	 *
44	 * `MarginCompensation` (below) additionally patches margin-left on BOTH
45	 * mechanisms to account for Obsidian's own "readable line width" feature,
46	 * which applies a `margin-inline: auto`-centering rule to every `.cm-line`
47	 * (any div child of `.cm-content`, in fact) — a *uniform* native base
48	 * margin our own `calc(depth * unit) !important` rule was silently
49	 * *replacing* instead of adding to, confirmed live (a depth-0 heading and
50	 * a depth-1 list item under it rendered with the list-item's own box to
51	 * the LEFT of the heading's, an inverted/negative-looking indentation —
52	 * reported by real-vault testing under a community theme with a narrower
53	 * reading column than the bundled themes, where the effect became large
54	 * enough to notice; the bug itself is present under any theme/viewport
55	 * where that base margin is nonzero, bundled themes included). See its
56	 * own doc comment below for the fix.
57	 */
58	
59	import { RangeSetBuilder, type Extension, type EditorState, type Text } from '@codemirror/state';
60	import {
61	  Decoration,
62	  EditorView,
63	  ViewPlugin,
64	  WidgetType,
65	  type DecorationSet,
66	  type PluginValue,
67	} from '@codemirror/view';
68	import { editorInfoField } from 'obsidian';
69	import type { NodeKind } from '../model';
70	import { parse } from '../parse';
71	import {
72	  computeLineGuides,
73	  decorate,
74	  type LineDecorationFact,
75	  type LineGuideFact,
76	} from './decorate';
77	import type { ModeSource } from './keymap';
78	
79	// ---- Shared per-document fact computation (hardening 5.4) ------------------
80	//
81	// All three ViewPlugins below need the same pure facts (parse → decorate →
82	// computeLineGuides) on every update. Each used to compute them
83	// independently — same asymptotics, tripled constant (the 2b baseline
84	// already did it twice; 5a added a third). Consolidated here into one
85	// computation cached by the *document* (`state.doc`, CM6's immutable `Text`
86	// instance): whichever plugin runs first on a given document pays the cost,
87	// every other consumer — and every subsequent non-doc update, where CM6
88	// reuses the same `Text` instance — gets the cached result. A WeakMap keyed
89	// on the `Text` itself (not a doc string) means no invalidation logic and
90	// no leak: entries die with the document they describe. Sound because the
91	// facts depend on nothing but the document text — mode gating and
92	// markerVisibility filtering both happen in the consumers, after this.
93	interface DocFacts {
94	  readonly facts: readonly LineDecorationFact[];
95	  readonly factsByLine: ReadonlyMap<number, LineDecorationFact>;
96	  readonly guides: readonly LineGuideFact[];
97	  readonly guidesByLine: ReadonlyMap<number, LineGuideFact>;
98	}
99	
100	const docFactsCache = new WeakMap<Text, DocFacts>();
101	
102	function docFacts(state: EditorState): DocFacts {
103	  const cached = docFactsCache.get(state.doc);
104	  if (cached) return cached;
105	  const doc = parse(state.doc.toString());
106	  const facts = decorate(doc);
107	  const guides = computeLineGuides(doc);
108	  const computed: DocFacts = {
109	    facts,
110	    factsByLine: new Map(facts.map((f) => [f.lineNumber, f])),
111	    guides,
112	    guidesByLine: new Map(guides.map((g) => [g.lineNumber, g])),
113	  };
114	  docFactsCache.set(state.doc, computed);
115	  return computed;
116	}
117	
118	// ---- Guide lines (Experiment 2b: CSS stacked-gradient) ---------------------
119	//
120	// @replit/codemirror-indentation-markers' technique: one `--to-guides`
121	// custom property per line, a comma-joined list of repeating-linear-
122	// gradient layers (one per active ancestor depth), consumed by a single
123	// `::before` — O(1) DOM nodes regardless of depth, no JS pixel measurement,
124	// no overlay layer (contrast Experiment 2a's measured overlay divs).
125	//
126	// `to-decor-atom`/`to-decor-list` lines use `margin-left` (Experiment 1's
127	// fix for the "padding never moves the box" bug), which DOES shift the
128	// line's own box — an earlier version of this code concluded from that
129	// alone that a guide could never render on those lines, since their own
130	// `::before` (position: absolute, relative to the line's own box) can't
131	// reach a shallower ancestor's column. That reasoning had a real bug: it
132	// assumed a background is clipped to the *positioned element's own box*,
133	// but the pseudo-element's own box does NOT have to match its containing
134	// block's dimensions — `left`/`right` can widen it arbitrarily, including
135	// leftward past where the line's box starts. Confirmed live (screenshot +
136	// computed style) that nothing in the ancestor chain up to `.cm-scroller`
137	// clips that overflow (`.cm-content`/`.cm-contentContainer`/`.cm-sizer`
138	// are all `overflow: visible`; `.cm-scroller` is `overflow: auto` but its
139	// own box starts well to the left of any guide column we'd ever need).
140	//
141	// The needed compensation ("how far has this line's own box been shifted
142	// right of the global column origin") is fully known at decoration-build
143	// time for both kinds, with NO live measurement: an atom's own shift is
144	// exactly `depth * unit` (our own margin-left value). A list item's own
145	// shift is exactly `supplementalDepth * unit` (our own margin-left value)
146	// too — confirmed live that Obsidian's native hang (`text-indent`/
147	// `padding-left`, applied to the very same `.cm-line`) contributes NOTHING
148	// to the box's own position: `getBoundingClientRect()` on a list line
149	// showed its box's left edge exactly matching its `margin-left`, despite a
150	// nonzero native `padding-left`/`text-indent` also being present — because
151	// neither property moves a box's own edges (padding shifts content only;
152	// text-indent shifts only the first inline line's content, per the CSS
153	// spec), only `margin` does. So both kinds can widen their own pseudo's
154	// box by exactly `--to-own-shift` units to reach any shallower ancestor's
155	// column, with no measurement beyond the JS constants this module already
156	// computes.
157	const UNIT = 'var(--to-decor-unit, 1.5rem)';
158	
159	function guideLayer(depth: number): string {
160	  return (
161	    `repeating-linear-gradient(to right, var(--text-faint) 0 1px, transparent 1px ${UNIT}) ` +
162	    `calc(${depth} * ${UNIT}) 0 / ${UNIT} 100% no-repeat`
163	  );
164	}
165	
166	function guideBackground(guideDepths: readonly number[]): string {
167	  return guideDepths.map(guideLayer).join(', ');
168	}
169	
170	// ---- Block markers (Experiment 5a: icon markers) ---------------------------
171	//
172	// See docs/research/10-experiment-5-block-markers.md (Experiment 5/5a). A
173	// small, distinct, self-drawn SVG icon per node kind, rendered on a node's
174	// own first line only (never a list item — the native bullet/number already
175	// does that job, same exclusion guides already use).
176	//
177	// Placement exploration (post-review) settled on the icon horizontally
178	// CENTERED on the guide-line column, with a marker gutter reserved
179	// additively so text still clears the icon (the other two candidates tried —
180	// icon's own left edge at the column, and no gutter with the icon's own
181	// right edge at the column — read worse in a real vault and were dropped).
182	// `MarkerVisibility` itself lives in mode-registry.ts (not here), so that
183	// pure, Obsidian-free module can keep defining PluginData without importing
184	// this one (which pulls in the real `obsidian` package for
185	// `editorInfoField`).
186	export type { MarkerVisibility } from './mode-registry';
187	import type { MarkerVisibility } from './mode-registry';
188	
189	/** Anything that can supply decorations needs to say which notes are in
190	 * outline mode (ModeSource) and which nodes get a marker at all — a real
191	 * Obsidian setting, read fresh on every recompute so switching it live (no
192	 * rebuild) takes effect on the very next transaction, the same way toggling
193	 * outline mode already does (see main.ts's refreshDecorations). */
194	export interface DecorationSource extends ModeSource {
195	  readonly markerVisibility: MarkerVisibility;
196	}
197	
198	/**
199	 * Whether a given node's marker should render at all (Experiment 5a
200	 * follow-up — see `MarkerVisibility`'s own doc comment in mode-registry.ts
201	 * for the reasoning). Deliberately does NOT touch the marker gutter
202	 * reservation (padding-left/margin-left) at all — that stays reserved
203	 * uniformly regardless of this setting, so hiding some markers never
204	 * reflows text/shifts indentation; only whether the icon itself is drawn
205	 * in that already-reserved space changes.
206	 */
207	function shouldShowMarker(fact: LineDecorationFact, visibility: MarkerVisibility): boolean {
208	  switch (visibility) {
209	    case 'all':
210	      return true;
211	    case 'with-children':
212	      return fact.hasChildren;
213	    case 'headings-and-paragraphs':
214	      // The only two marker-eligible kinds that can ever have children in
215	      // this tree model — atoms are leaves by construction (see hasChildren
216	      // itself), so `!fact.isAtom` is exactly "heading or paragraph."
217	      return !fact.isAtom;
218	  }
219	}
220	
221	const MARKER_GUTTER_REM = 1.25;
222	const MARKER_ICON_REM = 0.85;
223	// The actual CSS length emitted per line (see lineDecoration()) — a single
224	// source of truth the static CSS rules, the live margin overrides, AND the
225	// marker's own left-offset calc all agree with. Every non-list-item line
226	// reserves this gutter unconditionally (see lineDecoration()'s own
227	// reasoning for why list items don't).
228	const MARKER_GUTTER_CSS = `${MARKER_GUTTER_REM}rem`;
229	const MARKER_ICON_CSS = `${MARKER_ICON_REM}rem`;
230	
231	/**
232	 * Where a marker icon's own LEFT edge should sit, given `targetRelExpr` — a
233	 * CSS length expression for "the shared target column (where the guide for
234	 * this depth renders), relative to the box the marker is about to become a
235	 * child of" (see call sites: block/atom/widget-atom each derive this from
236	 * their own already-established `--to-own-shift`-style formula, so the
237	 * marker automatically stays correct if those formulas ever change). Used
238	 * only by the widget-atom mechanism below (table/callout/hr/html) — the
239	 * plain-line mechanism uses `MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR` instead (see its own
240	 * doc comment for why the two need different math). Centers the icon on the
241	 * target column (the placement exploration's winner — see the module doc
242	 * comment above).
243	 */
244	function markerAnchorLeftExpr(targetRelExpr: string): string {
245	  return `calc(${targetRelExpr} - (${MARKER_ICON_CSS} / 2))`;
246	}
247	
248	/**
249	 * Horizontal placement for the CM6-widget (plain-line) marker mechanism —
250	 * see `MarkerWidget`'s own doc comment for why this uses a fundamentally
251	 * different technique (inline + `vertical-align`, not `position: absolute`
252	 * relative to the line's own box) from the widget-atom mechanism above.
253	 *
254	 * Because the widget is always inserted at the exact position where the
255	 * node's own text starts (CM6 `Decoration.widget` at the line's first
256	 * character, `side: -1`), and — by construction — that text position is
257	 * always exactly `gutter` to the right of the shared target column,
258	 * regardless of kind or depth (that IS the definition of the gutter), the
259	 * needed shift collapses to a single depth/kind-independent expression:
260	 * `iconSize * 0.5 - gutter` (icon centered on the column). Worked through
261	 * concretely for both block (padding-shifted text, unshifted box) and
262	 * atom-plain (margin-shifted box, unshifted-relative-to-box text) — the
263	 * depth terms cancel identically in both cases, confirmed by hand before
264	 * relying on it here (see the git history of this comment for the full
265	 * derivation).
266	 */
267	const MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR = `calc(${MARKER_ICON_CSS} * 0.5 - ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS})`;
268	
269	const SVG_NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
270	
271	function svgEl<K extends keyof SVGElementTagNameMap>(
272	  tag: K,
273	  attrs: Record<string, string>,
274	): SVGElementTagNameMap[K] {
275	  const el = document.createElementNS(SVG_NS, tag);
276	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(attrs)) el.setAttribute(key, value);
277	  return el;
278	}
279	
280	const STROKE_ATTRS = {
281	  stroke: 'currentColor',
282	  'stroke-width': '1.5',
283	  fill: 'none',
284	  'stroke-linecap': 'round',
285	  'stroke-linejoin': 'round',
286	};
287	
288	/**
289	 * Builds one distinct, self-drawn SVG icon per eligible node kind — via DOM
290	 * APIs directly (element creation + attribute setting in code), not a
291	 * data-URI string. Not a final design; exact shapes are expected to be
292	 * tuned by eye during real-vault review, like every other visual call in
293	 * this project (see the plan's own framing of Experiment 5a).
294	 */
295	function buildMarkerIcon(kind: NodeKind): SVGSVGElement {
296	  // `aria-hidden`: the marker is purely decorative chrome (the node's kind
297	  // is already in the accessible text itself — heading level, code fence,
298	  // etc.), so screen readers should skip it entirely (hardening 5.6).
299	  const svg = svgEl('svg', {
300	    viewBox: '0 0 16 16',
301	    width: '100%',
302	    height: '100%',
303	    'aria-hidden': 'true',
304	  });
305	  const children: SVGElement[] = [];
306	
307	  switch (kind) {
308	    case 'heading':
309	      // A blocky "H": two vertical bars + a crossbar.
310	      children.push(
311	        svgEl('rect', { x: '3', y: '2', width: '2', height: '12', fill: 'currentColor' }),
312	        svgEl('rect', { x: '11', y: '2', width: '2', height: '12', fill: 'currentColor' }),
313	        svgEl('rect', { x: '3', y: '7', width: '10', height: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
314	      );
315	      break;
316	    case 'paragraph':
317	      // Three text lines, the last one shorter.
318	      children.push(
319	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '4', x2: '14', y2: '4' }),
320	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '8', x2: '14', y2: '8' }),
321	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '12', x2: '9', y2: '12' }),
322	      );
323	      break;
324	    case 'code':
325	      // "</>"
326	      children.push(
327	        svgEl('polyline', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, points: '6,3 2,8 6,13' }),
328	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '9.5', y1: '2', x2: '6.5', y2: '14' }),
329	        svgEl('polyline', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, points: '10,3 14,8 10,13' }),
330	      );
331	      break;
332	    case 'table':
333	      // 2x2 grid.
334	      children.push(
335	        svgEl('rect', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x: '2', y: '2', width: '12', height: '12', rx: '1' }),
336	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '2', y1: '8', x2: '14', y2: '8' }),
337	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '8', y1: '2', x2: '8', y2: '14' }),
338	      );
339	      break;
340	    case 'callout':
341	      // Filled alert circle with an "!" bar.
342	      children.push(
343	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '8', cy: '8', r: '6', fill: 'currentColor' }),
344	        svgEl('rect', { x: '7', y: '4', width: '2', height: '5', fill: 'var(--background-primary)' }),
345	        svgEl('rect', { x: '7', y: '10', width: '2', height: '2', fill: 'var(--background-primary)' }),
346	      );
347	      break;
348	    case 'quote':
349	      // Two opening-quote marks.
350	      children.push(
351	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '5', cy: '5', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
352	        svgEl('rect', { x: '4', y: '5', width: '2', height: '4', fill: 'currentColor' }),
353	        svgEl('circle', { cx: '11', cy: '5', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }),
354	        svgEl('rect', { x: '10', y: '5', width: '2', height: '4', fill: 'currentColor' }),
355	      );
356	      break;
357	    case 'html':
358	      // An outlined tag/document shape with a folded corner.
359	      children.push(
360	        svgEl('rect', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x: '3', y: '2', width: '10', height: '12', rx: '1' }),
361	        svgEl('line', { ...STROKE_ATTRS, x1: '9', y1: '2', x2: '13', y2: '6' }),
362	      );
363	      break;
364	    case 'hr':
365	      // A single bold horizontal bar.
366	      children.push(svgEl('rect', { x: '2', y: '7', width: '12', height: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }));
367	      break;
368	    default:
369	      // Unreachable for list-item (excluded by every caller) — a small dot
370	      // keeps this exhaustive-in-spirit without dead code paths elsewhere.
371	      children.push(svgEl('circle', { cx: '8', cy: '8', r: '2', fill: 'currentColor' }));
372	  }
373	
374	  // Safe DOM insertion (see the no-restricted-syntax guard in
375	  // eslint.config.js, hardening 5.2): `svg` is detached — built here, never
376	  // queried from the live document — so no CM6-owned or Obsidian-owned
377	  // subtree is being mutated.
378	  // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax -- detached DOM: built here, never mounted by this code
379	  svg.append(...children);
380	  return svg;
381	}
382	
383	/** Widget-replaced atom kinds (see the module doc comment) — markers on
384	 * these are injected directly by MarginCompensation, not the CM6 widget
385	 * below (a `Decoration` has zero effect on these elements). */
386	const WIDGET_ATOM_KINDS: ReadonlySet<NodeKind> = new Set(['table', 'callout', 'html', 'hr']);
387	
388	/**
389	 * "top:0; bottom:0; flex-center" positioning for the WIDGET-ATOM marker
390	 * only (table/callout/hr/html) — the plain-line marker (`MarkerWidget`,
391	 * above) uses a different, inline/`vertical-align` technique instead; see
392	 * its own doc comment for why. A widget atom IS its own single, opaque,
393	 * already-full-height DOM element (unlike a `.cm-line`, which can carry
394	 * extra margin/padding a naive "center in the whole box" approach would
395	 * wrongly include), so stretching to its own box height and flex-centering
396	 * is correct here without that risk. Deliberately NOT `top: 50%; transform:
397	 * translateY(-50%)` (an earlier version of this code used that and a
398	 * single-line paragraph's icon visibly sat lower than the text's own
399	 * center) — percentage/transform-based centering is sensitive to CSS
400	 * line-height/leading asymmetry; stretching the box to its full containing-
401	 * block height via `top`+`bottom` and centering the icon inside with flex
402	 * lets the browser's own box layout do the centering, unambiguously.
403	 *
404	 * Everything but `left` is a fixed constant, moved into the
405	 * `.to-decor-marker-icon`/`.to-decor-marker-icon--widget` CSS classes
406	 * (styles.css) per `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`'s `no-static-styles-assignment`
407	 * rule — `left` is the only value that genuinely varies per instance
408	 * (depth/kind-dependent), so it's the only one JS still sets, via
409	 * `setCssProps` onto the `--to-marker-left` custom property the class
410	 * references.
411	 */
412	function applyMarkerLeft(el: HTMLElement, leftExpr: string): void {
413	  el.setCssProps({ '--to-marker-left': leftExpr });
414	}
415	
416	class MarkerWidget extends WidgetType {
417	  constructor(
418	    private readonly kind: NodeKind,
419	    private readonly leftShiftExpr: string,
420	  ) {
421	    super();
422	  }
423	
424	  override eq(other: MarkerWidget): boolean {
425	    return other.kind === this.kind && other.leftShiftExpr === this.leftShiftExpr;
426	  }
427	
428	  /**
429	   * Deliberately NOT `position: absolute` (unlike the widget-atom marker
430	   * below) — an earlier version used `position: absolute; top: 0; bottom:
431	   * 0` relative to the LINE's own (padded) box, and a heading's marker sat
432	   * visibly too high, worse the bigger the heading. Root cause: a heading's
433	   * `.cm-line` box includes real, asymmetric spacing (more margin/padding
434	   * ABOVE the text than below, for visual breathing room from the
435	   * preceding block) that scales with heading level — centering within the
436	   * WHOLE box (spacing included) puts the icon above the text's own visual
437	   * center, more so for a bigger heading's bigger spacing. Fixed by staying
438	   * in NORMAL INLINE FLOW (`display: inline-block`), which aligns relative
439	   * to the surrounding TEXT's own font metrics (the actual heading text run
440	   * this widget sits next to) — completely blind to the outer block's own
441	   * margin/padding, by construction.
442	   *
443	   * `vertical-align: baseline` (the CSS default — set explicitly here so a
444	   * future reader doesn't need to know that), not `middle`: a real second
445	   * bug found in review — `middle` aligns THIS box's own vertical center to
446	   * "the parent's baseline + half the parent's x-height," a formula that
447	   * assumes the aligned box's own height is comparable to the surrounding
448	   * text's x-height. Our icon's height is a fixed, unrelated constant, so
449	   * that assumption doesn't hold — the mismatch put the icon consistently
450	   * low by roughly half its own height, on every kind, not just headings
451	   * (this time correctly *not* scaling with font size, since `middle`'s
452	   * error term doesn't depend on the wrapper's own fixed height — but still
453	   * visibly wrong). `baseline` instead aligns THIS element's own baseline —
454	   * for an inline-block whose only content is a single replaced child (the
455	   * SVG, itself baseline-aligned with no descender by default), that
456	   * resolves to the SVG's own bottom edge — to the surrounding text's
457	   * baseline. That's the same place a bare capital letter or a digit sits:
458	   * the natural, no-extra-math alignment for an icon meant to read as part
459	   * of the text, confirmed live against every heading level and paragraph.
460	   */
461	  toDOM(): HTMLElement {
462	    const wrapper = createSpan({ cls: 'to-decor-marker-icon' });
463	    applyMarkerLeft(wrapper, this.leftShiftExpr);
464	    // Safe DOM insertion (see the no-restricted-syntax guard in
465	    // eslint.config.js, hardening 5.2): `wrapper` is detached at this point
466	    // — CM6 itself mounts a widget's toDOM() result through its own
467	    // supported insertion path, which is the whole reason plain-line
468	    // markers use Decoration.widget instead of direct DOM injection.
469	    // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax -- detached DOM: CM6 mounts toDOM()'s result via its own supported path
470	    wrapper.appendChild(buildMarkerIcon(this.kind));
471	    return wrapper;
472	  }
473	
474	  override ignoreEvent(): boolean {
475	    return true;
476	  }
477	}
478	
479	function computeMarkers(state: EditorState, modes: DecorationSource): DecorationSet {
480	  const path = state.field(editorInfoField, false)?.file?.path;
481	  if (!path || !modes.isOutline(path)) return Decoration.none;
482	
483	  const totalLines = state.doc.lines;
484	  const builder = new RangeSetBuilder<Decoration>();
485	  for (const fact of docFacts(state).facts) {
486	    // List items keep their fully native marker, untouched (same exclusion
487	    // guides already use); continuation lines never repeat the marker.
488	    if (fact.isListItem || !fact.isFirstLine) continue;
489	    // Widget-replaced atoms have no plain `.cm-line` for a widget decoration
490	    // to attach to — MarginCompensation injects their marker directly.
491	    if (WIDGET_ATOM_KINDS.has(fact.kind)) continue;
492	    if (!shouldShowMarker(fact, modes.markerVisibility)) continue;
493	    if (fact.lineNumber >= totalLines) continue; // stale fact past a shrunk doc
494	
495	    const from = state.doc.line(fact.lineNumber + 1).from; // CM6 lines are 1-indexed
496	    builder.add(
497	      from,
498	      from,
499	      Decoration.widget({ widget: new MarkerWidget(fact.kind, MARKER_LEFT_SHIFT_EXPR), side: -1 }),
500	    );
501	  }
502	  return builder.finish();
503	}
504	
505	function lineDecoration(fact: LineDecorationFact, guide: LineGuideFact): Decoration {
506	  const styles: string[] = [];
507	  let cls: string;
508	  // Own-shift expression (units of `--to-decor-unit`, plus the marker
509	  // gutter where applicable) this line's own box has been shifted right by
510	  // its own margin-left — the exact compensation the guide's pseudo needs
511	  // to widen its box by, leftward, to reach a shallower ancestor's column
512	  // (see the doc comment above `guideLayer`). `undefined` means the box
513	  // isn't shifted at all (block lines: padding-left never moves the box).
514	  let ownShiftExpr: string | undefined;
515	
516	  if (fact.isListItem) {
517	    cls = 'to-decor-list';
518	    styles.push(`--to-supp-depth: ${fact.supplementalDepth}`);
519	    // List items get no marker gutter (native bullet/number only).
520	    ownShiftExpr = fact.supplementalDepth > 0 ? `calc(${fact.supplementalDepth} * ${UNIT})` : undefined;
521	  } else if (fact.isAtom) {
522	    cls = 'to-decor-atom';
523	    styles.push(`--to-depth: ${fact.depth}`);
524	    styles.push(`--to-marker-gutter: ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS}`);
525	    // Every non-list line reserves the marker gutter, so the box is always
526	    // shifted by at least the gutter, even at depth 0.
527	    ownShiftExpr = `calc(${fact.depth} * ${UNIT} + var(--to-marker-gutter, 0px))`;
528	  } else {
529	    cls = 'to-decor-block';
530	    styles.push(`--to-depth: ${fact.depth}`);
531	    styles.push(`--to-marker-gutter: ${MARKER_GUTTER_CSS}`);
532	    ownShiftExpr = undefined; // padding-left never shifts a block line's own box
533	  }
534	
535	  if (guide.guideDepths.length > 0) {
536	    cls += ' to-decor-guides';
537	    styles.push(`--to-guides: ${guideBackground(guide.guideDepths)}`);
538	    if (ownShiftExpr) {
539	      styles.push(`--to-own-shift: ${ownShiftExpr}`);
540	    }
541	  }
542	
543	  return Decoration.line({ class: cls, attributes: { style: styles.join('; ') } });
544	}
545	
546	// A blank trailingGap line carrying a guide (see computeLineGuides's doc
547	// comment) has no decorate() fact at all — no depth, no kind, nothing to
548	// indent — so it gets a minimal decoration with just the guide class/style,
549	// not the full lineDecoration() treatment.
550	function gapLineDecoration(guide: LineGuideFact): Decoration {
551	  return Decoration.line({
552	    class: 'to-decor-guides',
553	    attributes: { style: `--to-guides: ${guideBackground(guide.guideDepths)}` },
554	  });
555	}
556	
557	function computeDecorations(state: EditorState, modes: DecorationSource): DecorationSet {
558	  const path = state.field(editorInfoField, false)?.file?.path;
559	  if (!path || !modes.isOutline(path)) return Decoration.none;
560	
561	  // computeLineGuides is a strict superset of decorate() by line coverage
562	  // (every line decorate() covers, plus gap-only lines) — iterate it as
563	  // the primary sequence (still ascending by lineNumber, required by
564	  // RangeSetBuilder) and look up the matching decorate() fact by line
565	  // number instead of assuming index alignment, since gap lines have no
566	  // corresponding entry there at all.
567	  const { factsByLine, guides } = docFacts(state);
568	  const totalLines = state.doc.lines;
569	  const builder = new RangeSetBuilder<Decoration>();
570	  for (const guide of guides) {
571	    if (guide.lineNumber >= totalLines) continue; // stale fact past a shrunk doc
572	    const from = state.doc.line(guide.lineNumber + 1).from; // CM6 lines are 1-indexed
573	    if (guide.isGapLine) {
574	      if (guide.guideDepths.length === 0) continue; // nothing to draw
575	      builder.add(from, from, gapLineDecoration(guide));
576	      continue;
577	    }
578	    const fact = factsByLine.get(guide.lineNumber);
579	    if (!fact) continue; // decorate()/computeLineGuides walks are in sync; defensive only
580	    builder.add(from, from, lineDecoration(fact, guide));
581	  }
582	  return builder.finish();
583	}
584	
585	// Obsidian's own selectors for widget-replaced atom kinds — matches tables,
586	// callouts, and raw HTML blocks (`.cm-embed-block`) plus horizontal rules
587	// (`.hr`, which oddly also carries `cm-line` but is widget-rendered all the
588	// same). Broad by design: elements it catches that don't correspond to an
589	// atom fact (e.g. an inline image embed inside a paragraph) just get their
590	// margin cleared, a no-op.
591	const WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR = '.cm-embed-block, .cm-line.hr';
592	
593	// Plain `.cm-line`s that carry one of our own margin-based decorations
594	// (atoms, list items) — needs the SAME native-base compensation as
595	// widgets, for the same reason (see MarginCompensation's doc comment).
596	const PLAIN_MARGIN_SELECTOR = '.cm-line.to-decor-atom, .cm-line.to-decor-list';
597	
598	/**
599	 * Injects/updates the marker icon child on a widget-replaced atom element
600	 * (table/callout/html/hr) — the only way to reach these (Experiment 5a),
601	 * same reasoning as the margin-left patch above: a CM6 decoration has no
602	 * effect on these elements at all. Idempotent: skips the rebuild when the
603	 * kind/position hasn't changed, so a `docViewUpdate` on every render
604	 * doesn't thrash the DOM. Appending a child directly into one of these
605	 * widgets is safe — see the module doc comment for why this is NOT safe to
606	 * do for a plain `.cm-line`.
607	 *
608	 * `ownShiftExpr` is the EXACT expression this widget's own margin-left was
609	 * just computed with (below) — deriving the marker's target column FROM
610	 * that shared expression (`depth*unit - ownShiftExpr`), rather than
611	 * assuming a simplified `-gutter` shortcut, is what fixes a real bug: an
612	 * earlier version used the simplified shortcut for every atom-like kind,
613	 * which happened to match for code/quote (no native-padding correction)
614	 * but silently diverged for tables — whose `ownShiftExpr` ALSO subtracts
615	 * the table widget's own native cell padding (see the margin-left comment
616	 * below) — visibly offsetting the table's marker from every other kind's
617	 * marker at the same depth.
618	 */
619	function applyWidgetMarker(el: HTMLElement, kind: NodeKind, ownShiftExpr: string): void {
620	  const targetRelExpr = `calc(${el.dataset.markerDepth ?? '0'} * ${UNIT} - (${ownShiftExpr}))`;
621	  const leftExpr = markerAnchorLeftExpr(targetRelExpr);
622	  const existing = el.querySelector<HTMLElement>(':scope > .to-decor-marker-icon');
623	  if (existing) {
624	    applyMarkerLeft(existing, leftExpr);
625	    if (existing.dataset.kind === kind) return;
626	    existing.remove();
627	  }
628	  const icon = createSpan({ cls: 'to-decor-marker-icon to-decor-marker-icon--widget' });
629	  icon.dataset.kind = kind;
630	  applyMarkerLeft(icon, leftExpr);
631	  // Safe DOM insertion: `icon` is still detached here.
632	  // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax -- detached DOM: icon not yet mounted
633	  icon.appendChild(buildMarkerIcon(kind));
634	  // THE sanctioned live-DOM injection site (invariant (a), see the module
635	  // doc comment and the no-restricted-syntax guard in eslint.config.js):
636	  // `el` is a widget-replaced atom (matched by WIDGET_ATOM_SELECTOR only) —
637	  // an opaque, Obsidian-owned subtree CM6 never re-diffs internally, which
638	  // is what makes this append safe where the same call on a plain
639	  // `.cm-line` pegs the renderer. If Obsidian ever starts re-diffing these
640	  // subtrees, the failure mode is re-injection flicker/duplicated markers —
641	  // the idempotence guard above (kind/position skip) is the first line of
642	  // defense, and the duplicate-marker e2e test the second.
643	  // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax -- sanctioned widget-atom injection (invariant (a), see comment above)
644	  el.prepend(icon);
645	}
646	
647	function clearWidgetMarker(el: HTMLElement): void {
648	  el.querySelector(':scope > .to-decor-marker-icon')?.remove();
649	  el.classList.remove('to-decor-marker');
650	  delete el.dataset.markerDepth;
651	}
